input.background: An investigation is being conducted to study the possible effects on non-smokers of allowing smoking in a shared office environment. The goal is to determine which statistical test is most appropriate to identify differences in the central tendency of the samples collected under different scenarios.
input.question: Which statistical test would you employ if the investigators had measured the lung capacity (in cubic metres) of the non-smokers before smoking was banned, and on the same staff a month after a ban on smoking had been enforced?

output.answer: Ideally a paired $t$ test would be employed (a Wilcoxon test could be used, but is more conservative when data are likely to be normally distributed).

meta_info.book: Using Statistics to Understand the Environment
